page.content: The table presents the antimicrobial effectiveness of various microorganisms as indicated by the zone of inhibition measured in millimeters across different concentrations and a control. For Gram positive microorganisms, Bacillus subtilis exhibited zones of inhibition of 20 mm at 1000 μg, 18 mm at 500 μg, and 12 mm at 250 μg, with no measurable inhibition at 125 μg and 62.5 μg, and no data for DMSO. Staphylococcus epidermidis showed inhibition of 15 mm at 1000 μg, 10 mm at 500 μg, with no inhibition recorded at 250 μg, 125 μg, and 62.5 μg, and no data for DMSO. Enterococcus faecalis demonstrated a zone of inhibition of 10 mm at 1000 μg, with no inhibition at other concentrations or DMSO. The Gram negative microorganisms presented varied results; Escherichia coli had zones of inhibition of 25 mm at 1000 μg, 21 mm at 500 μg, 13 mm at 250 μg, and 7 mm at 125 μg, with no data for 62.5 μg and DMSO. Klebsiella pneumoniae showed 15 mm at 1000 μg, 12 mm at 500 μg, 10 mm at 250 μg, and 7 mm at 125 μg, with no data for 62.5 μg and DMSO.

lines.text: The table presents the antimicrobial effectiveness of various microorganisms as indicated by the zone of inhibition measured in
millimeters across different concentrations and a control. For Gram positive microorganisms, Bacillus subtilis exhibited zones
of inhibition of 20 mm at 1000 μg, 18 mm at 500 μg, and 12 mm at 250 μg, with no measurable inhibition at 125 μg and 62.5
μg, and no data for DMSO. Staphylococcus epidermidis showed inhibition of 15 mm at 1000 μg, 10 mm at 500 μg, with no
inhibition recorded at 250 μg, 125 μg, and 62.5 μg, and no data for DMSO. Enterococcus faecalis demonstrated a zone of
inhibition of 10 mm at 1000 μg, with no inhibition at other concentrations or DMSO. The Gram negative microorganisms
presented varied results; Escherichia coli had zones of inhibition of 25 mm at 1000 μg, 21 mm at 500 μg, 13 mm at 250 μg, and
7 mm at 125 μg, with no data for 62.5 μg and DMSO. Klebsiella pneumoniae showed 15 mm at 1000 μg, 12 mm at 500 μg, 10
mm at 250 μg, and 7 mm at 125 μg, with no data for 62.5 μg and DMSO.

page.content: Pseudomonas aeruginosa had 21 mm at 1000 μg, 20 mm at 500 μg, 12 mm at 250 μg, 10 mm at 125 μg, and 7 mm at 62.5 μg, with no data for DMSO. Finally, Salmonella typhi exhibited inhibition of 22 mm at 1000 μg, 20 mm at 500 μg, 15 mm at 250 μg, 9 mm at 125 μg, 7 mm at 62.5 μg, and no data for DMSO. Proteus mirabilis showed no measurable inhibition across all tested concentrations and DMSO.
